parsed_review.summary: CP00-691 is a descriptor-only RP22 External Integrations I P09 'Secondary Workflow Slice' (M04) + 'Permission And Audit Binding' (M05) bridge pack, 40 units (RP22.P09.M04.S01-RP22.P09.M05.S20), risk B. It correctly claims production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false and is in pending_review state awaiting exactly one hardened read-only Claude review (this receipt); no over-claim of runtime or enterprise trust. VERIFIED BY READ-ONLY INSPECTION: (1) registry.js:1659-1758 CP691 binding+requirements match the baseline exactly - pack_id CP00-691, risk B, 40 units, range matches, upstream CP00-690, next CP00-692/RP22.P09.M05.S21; deliverable counts {claude_review:6, security_audit:4, test:2, ui:2, implementation:26}=40, phase {RP22.P09:40}, micro-phase {M04:20,M05:20}, micro-title {Secondary Workflow Slice:20, Permission And Audit Binding:20} - independently re-counted from the 40 baseline units and consistent. (2) service.js:3429-3475 case-set/descriptor/hermes/claude/handoff factories are descriptor-only: every row descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false; freezeCp691Result (597-605) stamps the full no-write/no-real-data attestation (writes_product_state=false, no_real_data=true, evaluates_runtime_permission=false, writes_audit_event=false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, etc.); authority_boundary keeps claude_is_final_approval=false and human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true; hermes_packet hermes_runtime_opened=false and runtime_receipt_emitted=false (Hermes used only for deterministic control-plane packet/receipt labeling, not product runtime). (3) validators.js:1051-1067 + 2161-2174 enforce coverage distributions and assert all descriptor-only flags true and all permission/audit/security/ui/persistence/command/hermes/claude runtime-opened flags false, plus claude-not-final and external_api/credential/permission/audit runtime boundaries closed. (4) model.test.js:672-683 + 1302-1329 assert coverage matches the live plan distribution and the descriptor bridges P09 rows without runtime, handoff to CP00-692/RP22.P09.M05.S21; projection test 1331-1398 asserts current_pack=CP00-691 with historical_pack_ids through CP00-690. (5) index.js re-exports via `export *`; validate-rp22-external-integrations-i-contract.mjs threads CP691 through binding/coverage/descriptor/hermes/claude/handoff/projection, and both contracts/integrations-core-contract.json and contracts/external-integrations-i-contract.json are generated_from_pack_id=CP00-691 and consistent. (6) Evidence files agree: manifest production_ready=false/runtime_ready=false/descriptor_only=true; command-evidence writes_product_state=false/no_real_data=true with 22 commands recorded exit 0; construction-inspection and adjudication consistently pending review. REVIEW HARDENING (CP178+): readiness auth preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON result, 1441-byte stdout, no stderr - not an auth-failure/0-byte/malformed case); validation-report confirms prompt 2456B < 25600B, no full diff/source (read_tools_used), summary-only prohibited for risk B with security/audit+review deliverables, schema present, no forbidden tools, no active untracked implementation files omitted. No secrets/credentials/raw payloads found in changed sources or pack evidence. AUTHORITY BOUNDARY preserved: Claude remains a read-only, non-final reviewer; enterprise-trust closed outside this receipt; the pack does not itself claim runtime or enterprise trust, so blocks_goal_closeout=false. PROVENANCE CAVEAT: the 22 pre-review validation commands are asserted exit 0 in command-evidence and are fully consistent with the source/tests/validators I inspected, but were NOT independently re-executed in this read-only (Read/Grep/Glob) review. One non-blocking P3 transparency note on coverage-validation topology is recorded below; no P0/P1/P2 issues.

findings.title: Live pack coverage resolved via manifest plan_binding_snapshot rather than an independent plan-array entry
findings.details: The live docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json carries CP00-691 only in live_state (line 36); its packs[] array begins at CP00-692 (line 402). As a result both the contract-validation script (validate-rp22-external-integrations-i-contract.mjs:359-361) and the model test (packages/integrations-core/test/model.test.js:349-351) resolve the CP00-691 plan pack through the `?? cp691Manifest?.plan_binding_snapshot` fallback, so the live pack's coverage is checked against a snapshot embedded in the pack's own manifest rather than an independent plan-array entry. Risk is low: validateExternalIntegrationsICp691Coverage (validators.js:1051-1067) checks that snapshot against the frozen EXTERNAL_INTEGRATIONS_I_CP691_REQUIREMENTS constants hard-coded in registry.js:1676-1758 (deliverable/phase/micro-phase/micro-title counts, independently re-verified to sum to 40), which serve as the code-reviewed anchor, and the manifest snapshot shares the live plan's source_ledger_sha (13c7ed65...) indicating common generator provenance. This is the same topology recorded as a non-blocking P3 in the CP00-690 review.
findings.recommendation: No change required for closeout. Optionally, a future generator run could emit the current/live pack into plan.packs[] (or a dedicated closed-packs array) so the live pack's coverage is cross-validated against a plan-level entry independent of its own manifest.
findings.post_review_disposition: Accepted as expected, low-risk design; non-blocking. The frozen REQUIREMENTS constants in registry.js are the independent coverage anchor and the manifest snapshot shares the live plan's ledger SHA, so the verification chain remains sound.
